title: carex ferruginea subsp. tenax
intro: Carex ferruginea subsp. tenax, also known as rusty sedge, is a type of perennial sedge plant native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits associated with Carex ferruginea subsp. tenax include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing inflammation and improving urinary function. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
